facts.text: DeSoto Acres was founded by William Van Dame, a Chicago businessman who moved to Sarasota in 1923.
The community began as Van Dame Estates, legally established on Feb 3, 1921, and renamed DeSoto Acres when re-platted on March 5, 1945.
Covering over 850 acres, DeSoto Acres is the largest one-house-per-two-acres low‑density residential area in Sarasota County.
Horses are still part of the neighborhood, making DeSoto Acres unique in Sarasota County as the oldest subdivision outside city limits where equestrian activity remains common.
The earliest home dates from 1925, while the most recent was built in 2020, showing the community’s long building span.
Native and migrating wildlife such as bald eagles, ospreys, sandhill cranes, and even occasional bobcats roam the lots, giving residents a front‑row seat to nature.
